items.md: Work permit means a certified procedure for allowing certain work to be carried out in a fixed place and during a given period of time. It is also a procedure authorizing entry to certain facilities. It provides a check list for a systematic method of risk assessment, warns the residual hazards in the job, and outlines the precautions to be taken while carrying out the job to achieve the stated objectives. It provides responsibility for the persons who are accountable for authorizing the work and ensuring that the workplace is safe. It establishes safe working conditions for carrying out any maintenance/repair/inspection work, and to serve as an official record of those established conditions as requirements agreed upon by the issuer and acceptor of the permit.

It provides responsibility for the persons who are accountable for authorizing the work and ensuring that the workplace is safe. It establishes safe working conditions for carrying out any maintenance/repair/inspection work, and to serve as an official record of those established conditions as requirements agreed upon by the issuer and acceptor of the permit.

items.md: Work permit is used to authorize routine/non-routine work to be carried out. It clearly explains the precautions to be taken to ensure safety of personnel, installations and environment while performing the job.
items.value: Work permit is used to authorize routine/non-routine work to be carried out. It clearly explains the precautions to be taken to ensure safety of personnel, installations and environment while performing the job.

items.md: This standard provides guidance for preparation of work permit for work areas whose activities are restrictive in nature such as 'cold work', 'hot work', 'radiography' etc. There is no international standard on this subject. items.md: ## 2. GENERAL PROCEDURE FOR WORK PERMIT
items.value: 2. GENERAL PROCEDURE FOR WORK PERMIT

items.md: The process of work permit involves adherence to specific procedures for issuance of work permit, compliance of agreed conditions in the permit during execution and closure of permit when the job is completed.
items.value: The process of work permit involves adherence to specific procedures for issuance of work permit, compliance of agreed conditions in the permit during execution and closure of permit when the job is completed.

items.md: The process involves different category of people in connection with work permit such as: issuer, acceptor/executor, area in-charge, area operator/technician, gas tester, maintenance technician/contract supervisor, etc.
items.value: The process involves different category of people in connection with work permit such as: issuer, acceptor/executor, area in-charge, area operator/technician, gas tester, maintenance technician/contract supervisor, etc.

items.md: The issuer is responsible for and controls the entire work permit process.
items.value: The issuer is responsible for and controls the entire work permit process.

items.md: Work Permit is to be made in triplicate. The first copy is the acceptors/executors copy, and the acceptor shall display his copy at the job location to make the area personnel aware of the job in progress and is readily available for audit. The issuer retains the second copy for control of work activities and for record purpose and the third copy is for safety department.
items.value: Work Permit is to be made in triplicate. The first copy is the acceptors/executors copy, and the acceptor shall display his copy at the job location to make the area personnel aware of the job in progress and is readily available for audit. The issuer retains the second copy for control of work activities and for record purpose and the third copy is for safety department.

items.md: A request for work permit comes from either the issuer or the acceptor. The issuer is normally the shift in-charge of the operations area and the acceptor is the person who carries out the work and has responsibility for the execution of work.
items.value: A request for work permit comes from either the issuer or the acceptor. The issuer is normally the shift in-charge of the operations area and the acceptor is the person who carries out the work and has responsibility for the execution of work.

items.md: ## 3.1 Cold Work
items.value: 3.1 Cold Work

items.value: 3.1.1 Cold Work is an activity, which is not likely to produce sufficient heat capable of igniting flammable gases. Annex A shall be followed to issue cold work permit.

items.md: b) Insulation of equipment/pipelines;

items.md: ### 3.2.1 Hot work is any type of work which produces heat/spark capable of igniting any flammable or combustible material. Annex B shall be followed to issue Hot Work Permit.

items.md: ### 3.3 Confined space/Vessel entry
items.value: 3.3 Confined space/Vessel entry

items.md: **3.3.1** A confined space is defined as an area which is not designed for continuous human occupancy. It is large enough for a person to bodily enter in the area and perform the assigned work. It has got limited or restricted means for entry and exit and may or may not be enclosed. Annex C shall be followed to issue confined space entry permit.
items.value: 3.3.1 A confined space is defined as an area which is not designed for continuous human occupancy. It is large enough for a person to bodily enter in the area and perform the assigned work. It has got limited or restricted means for entry and exit and may or may not be enclosed. Annex C shall be followed to issue confined space entry permit.

items.md: **3.3.2** Confined space is subjected to potential hazards, namely, accumulation of toxic or flammable gas/vapour, inert gas, oxygen deficiency (< 20 percent v/v), flooding of liquid, steam or any other hazardous substance or development of hazardous conditions.
items.value: 3.3.2 Confined space is subjected to potential hazards, namely, accumulation of toxic or flammable gas/vapour, inert gas, oxygen deficiency (< 20 percent v/v), flooding of liquid, steam or any other hazardous substance or development of hazardous conditions.

items.md:
**3.3.3** Some of the examples of confined spaces are as follows:
a) Tanks, columns, reactors, oil/water sewer pits, coolers, condensers, reservoirs, pipe lines, tanker trucks, skirts of column and vessels, floating roof decks, ducts, pits, tunnels, etc; and
b) This also includes column skirts, large diameter pipelines, storage tanks, spheres, bullets, bins, silos, sumps, trenches, pits, sewers, furnaces, flare stacks, gas turbine chambers, underground service lines, work in open type vessel deeper than head level, boiler drums, surface condensers, flue ducts, Heating ventilation and air conditioning ducts, false ceilings and floors, deck of floating roof tank etc, where deficiency of oxygen or presence of toxic/flammable gases is likely and/or the access is difficult during maintenance/inspection work.

items.md: ### 4.1.4.1 Process Isolation Methods
items.value: 4.1.4.1 Process Isolation Methods

items.md: The process isolations can be achieved by the following methods:
items.value: The process isolations can be achieved by the following methods:

items.md: a) By closing isolation valve tight shut and keeping drain/vent valve open between the equipment and isolation valve;
items.value: a) By closing isolation valve tight shut and keeping drain/vent valve open between the equipment and isolation valve;

items.md: b) By closing the double block valves and keeping the in between bleed system open;
items.value: b) By closing the double block valves and keeping the in between bleed system open;

items.md: c) By closing isolation valve tight shut and inserting blinding of same classification as the pipeline or the equipment;
items.value: c) By closing isolation valve tight shut and inserting blinding of same classification as the pipeline or the equipment;

items.md: d) By physical disconnection of pipelines attached to the equipment and blinding the open end (live end); and
items.value: d) By physical disconnection of pipelines attached to the equipment and blinding the open end (live end); and

items.md: e) By closing the motor operated valve (MOV) and isolating power supply to the drive motor of MOV.
items.value: e) By closing the motor operated valve (MOV) and isolating power supply to the drive motor of MOV.

items.md: A trained standby person must be always available at manhole of the tank during confined space entry. Requirement of standby fireman is to be decided by control room shift in-charge. For all work involving confined space/vessel entry, one person is required to be on guard outside the confined space/vessel, for help or in case of any distress call raised by persons working inside. It is the duty of the standby person to arrange for the rescue or raise the call for help. In case a hot work is carried out in a hazardous area, one person trained in fire-fighting should be posted near the work area. The permit issuer provides the necessary standby persons for above cases. The name of such standby persons should be mentioned in the work permit.

items.md: ### A-6 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR COLD WORK PERMIT
items.value: A-6 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR COLD WORK PERMIT

items.md: **A-6.1** The work permit shall be filled up carefully and accurately in clear handwriting ensuring that complete information is provided in all the sections/subsections and none of column is left blank. Sketches should be provided wherever possible to avoid miscommunication.
items.value: A-6.1 The work permit shall be filled up carefully and accurately in clear handwriting ensuring that complete information is provided in all the sections/subsections and none of column is left blank. Sketches should be provided wherever possible to avoid miscommunication.

items.md: **A-6.2** Appropriate safe guards and required personnel protective equipment (PPEs) shall be determined by a careful analysis of the potential hazards and the operations to be performed prior to starting the work.
items.value: A-6.2 Appropriate safe guards and required personnel protective equipment (PPEs) shall be determined by a careful analysis of the potential hazards and the operations to be performed prior to starting the work.

items.md: **A-6.3** Requirement of standby personnel from process/maintenance/contractor/fire and safety etc, if any shall be mentioned in the additional requirement.
items.value: A-6.3 Requirement of standby personnel from process/maintenance/contractor/fire and safety etc, if any shall be mentioned in the additional requirement.

items.md: **A-6.4** In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency sirens, all work must be stopped and all nonessential personnel must leave work site and processed to designated areas.
items.value: A-6.4 In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency sirens, all work must be stopped and all nonessential personnel must leave work site and processed to designated areas.

items.md: **A-6.5** For renewal of work clearance, the issuer shall ensure that the conditions are satisfactory for the work to continue. If the conditions have changed, it may be necessary to issue a new permit or amend the existing permit.
items.value: A-6.5 For renewal of work clearance, the issuer shall ensure that the conditions are satisfactory for the work to continue. If the conditions have changed, it may be necessary to issue a new permit or amend the existing permit.

items.md: **A-6.6** This permit shall be valid for a period of 8 hours only, unless it is renewed/extended. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days.
items.value: A-6.6 This permit shall be valid for a period of 8 hours only, unless it is renewed/extended. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days.

items.md: **A-6.7** Original permit must be available at work site at all times.
items.value: A-6.7 Original permit must be available at work site at all times.

items.md: **A-6.8** On completion of the work, the permit shall be closed and returned to the issuer.
items.value: A-6.8 On completion of the work, the permit shall be closed and returned to the issuer.

items.md: **A-6.9** The permit shall be issued in duplicate. Original permit (yellow) shall be issued by receiver/executer and duplicate (white) shall be retained by issuer.
items.value: A-6.9 The permit shall be issued in duplicate. Original permit (yellow) shall be issued by receiver/executer and duplicate (white) shall be retained by issuer.

items.md: **A-6.10** Enclose important Do’s and Don’ts duly signed with each permit.
items.value: A-6.10 Enclose important Do’s and Don’ts duly signed with each permit.

items.md: **A-6.11** Safety toolbox talk has to be ensured by receiver before start of work and record for the same shall be maintained.
items.value: A-6.11 Safety toolbox talk has to be ensured by receiver before start of work and record for the same shall be maintained.

items.md: **A-6.12** Obtain separate permit for “working at height”, if required.
items.value: A-6.12 Obtain separate permit for “working at height”, if required.

items.value: A-7 DO’S AND DON’TS FOR COLD WORK

A-7.1 Do’s

items.md: **A-7.1.1** Ensure the availability of valid work permit before start of work.
items.value: A-7.1.1 Ensure the availability of valid work permit before start of work.

items.md: **A-7.1.2** Ensure that work permit conditions are fully complied at site.
items.value: A-7.1.2 Ensure that work permit conditions are fully complied at site.

items.md: **A-7.1.3** Equipment should be properly isolated from all sources of energy before start of work.
items.value: A-7.1.3 Equipment should be properly isolated from all sources of energy before start of work.

items.md: **A-7.1.4** Ensure that walkways and passages are free from all slip/trip and fall hazard.
items.value: A-7.1.4 Ensure that walkways and passages are free from all slip/trip and fall hazard.

items.md: **A-7.1.5** All draining of oil should be in closed system as the draining of oil on floor will make the work area and area around the work unsafe.
items.value: A-7.1.5 All draining of oil should be in closed system as the draining of oil on floor will make the work area and area around the work unsafe.

items.md: **A-7.1.6** Ensure proper illumination of work place while working in dark.
items.value: A-7.1.6 Ensure proper illumination of work place while working in dark.

items.md: **A-7.1.7** Ensure use of non-sparking tools in flammable areas.
items.value: A-7.1.7 Ensure use of non-sparking tools in flammable areas.

items.md: **A-7.1.8** Executor should ensure the quality of hand tools and their health.
items.value: A-7.1.8 Executor should ensure the quality of hand tools and their health.

**A-7.1.9** Always first wedge open the flanges.
items.value: A-7.1.9 Always first wedge open the flanges.

items.md: **A-7.1.10** Use of proper PPE must be ensured during cold work by executor. Use of safety helmet and shoe is mandatory for all works inside plant.
items.value: A-7.1.10 Use of proper PPE must be ensured during cold work by executor. Use of safety helmet and shoe is mandatory for all works inside plant.

items.md: **A-7.1.11** While carrying out cold work inside confined space, proper ventilation and illumination should be ensured before start of job.
items.value: A-7.1.11 While carrying out cold work inside confined space, proper ventilation and illumination should be ensured before start of job.

items.md: **A-7.1.12** Electric cable should be free from joints with sound insulation. Portable electrical equipment should have no loose connections.
items.value: A-7.1.12 Electric cable should be free from joints with sound insulation. Portable electrical equipment should have no loose connections.

items.md: **A-7.1.13** All portable/mobile electrical equipment should be connected with ELCB.
items.value: A-7.1.13 All portable/mobile electrical equipment should be connected with ELCB.

items.md: **A-7.1.14** Executor should ensure that the lifting machine (crane), tools and tackles are properly tested and SWL and date of testing is displayed on equipment.
items.value: A-7.1.14 Executor should ensure that the lifting machine (crane), tools and tackles are properly tested and SWL and date of testing is displayed on equipment.

items.md: **A-7.1.15** Barricading of the area below lifting machine (crane), chain pulley blocks etc, should be ensured before starting the job.
items.value: A-7.1.15 Barricading of the area below lifting machine (crane), chain pulley blocks etc, should be ensured before starting the job.

items.md: **A-7.1.16** Gas test should be carried out before issuing cold work permit, wherever required.
items.value: A-7.1.16 Gas test should be carried out before issuing cold work permit, wherever required.

items.md: **A-7.1.17** Remove all scraps and unused material from site on completion of work.
items.value: A-7.1.17 Remove all scraps and unused material from site on completion of work.

items.md: **A-7.1.18** Executor should know the nearest fire alarm point, fire order, fire station phone number, first-aid center phone no., nearest safety shower, escape route and location of designated assembly points and siren code before start of work.
items.value: A-7.1.18 Executor should know the nearest fire alarm point, fire order, fire station phone number, first-aid center phone no., nearest safety shower, escape route and location of designated assembly points and siren code before start of work.

items.md: **A-7.2.1** Never stand or work under suspended loads.

items.value: A-7.2.2 Never enter work area without safety helmet and shoe.

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
**B-6 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS HOT WORK AND CONFINED SPACE ENTRY PERMIT**
items.value: B-6 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS HOT WORK AND CONFINED SPACE ENTRY PERMIT

items.md: **B-6.1** Work permit shall be filled up carefully and no column shall be left blank. Provide sketches, if required.
items.value: B-6.1 Work permit shall be filled up carefully and no column shall be left blank. Provide sketches, if required.

items.md: **B-6.2** Appropriate safe guards and PPEs shall be determined by analysis of the potential hazards prior to starting the work.
items.value: B-6.2 Appropriate safe guards and PPEs shall be determined by analysis of the potential hazards prior to starting the work.

items.md: **B-6.3** In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency sirens, all work must be stopped and all non-essential personnel must leave work site and proceed to designated areas.
items.value: B-6.3 In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency sirens, all work must be stopped and all non-essential personnel must leave work site and proceed to designated areas.

items.md: **B-6.4** Only certified vehicle/engines and permitted type of electrical equipment and tools are allowed in operating areas.
items.value: B-6.4 Only certified vehicle/engines and permitted type of electrical equipment and tools are allowed in operating areas.

items.md: **B-6.5** Welding machines should be located in non-hazardous and ventilated areas.
items.value: B-6.5 Welding machines should be located in non-hazardous and ventilated areas.

items.md: **B-6.6** Gas test is mandatory. No hot work shall be permitted unless the explosive meter reading is zero.
items.value: B-6.6 Gas test is mandatory. No hot work shall be permitted unless the explosive meter reading is zero.

items.md: **B-6.7** Vessel entry, where no hot work is to be carried out, shall be permitted if combustible gases are up to 5 percent of lower explosive limit \[LEL]. Entry with an air-supplied mask shall be permitted with LEL of up to 20 percent. The Oxygen level shall be at least 19.5 percent volume, and the concentration of toxic gases below the threshold limits.
items.value: B-6.7 Vessel entry, where no hot work is to be carried out, shall be permitted if combustible gases are up to 5 percent of lower explosive limit [LEL]. Entry with an air-supplied mask shall be permitted with LEL of up to 20 percent. The Oxygen level shall be at least 19.5 percent volume, and the concentration of toxic gases below the threshold limits.

items.md: **B-6.8** When a person is entering confined space, the receiver must keep minimum two trained attendants at the manhole or entry point.
items.value: B-6.8 When a person is entering confined space, the receiver must keep minimum two trained attendants at the manhole or entry point.

items.md: **B-6.9** For renewal of work clearance, the issuer shall ensure that the conditions are satisfactory for the work to continue. If the conditions have changed, it should be necessary to issue a new permit or amend the existing permit.
items.value: B-6.9 For renewal of work clearance, the issuer shall ensure that the conditions are satisfactory for the work to continue. If the conditions have changed, it should be necessary to issue a new permit or amend the existing permit.

items.md: **B-6.10** Permit shall be considered issued for job after "clearance renewal" is signed. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days, except in case of plant shutdown, where permit can be issued for a maximum period of 15 days. Blanket permit may be issued for a maximum period of 30 days.
items.value: B-6.10 Permit shall be considered issued for job after "clearance renewal" is signed. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days, except in case of plant shutdown, where permit can be issued for a maximum period of 15 days. Blanket permit may be issued for a maximum period of 30 days.

items.md: **B-6.11** During the registration period of permit, if job is not done for a day or more (except holidays/sundays), receiver shall enter in prescribed register at fire station in advance about the discontinuity of job.
items.value: B-6.11 During the registration period of permit, if job is not done for a day or more (except holidays/sundays), receiver shall enter in prescribed register at fire station in advance about the discontinuity of job.

items.md: **B-6.12** This permit must be available at work site at all times.
items.value: B-6.12 This permit must be available at work site at all times.

items.md: **B-6.13** On completion of the work, the permit must be closed and submitted to F and S as record by receiver.
items.value: B-6.13 On completion of the work, the permit must be closed and submitted to F and S as record by receiver.

items.md: **B-6.14** Hot work shall include welding, grinding, gas cutting, burning, open flame, soldering, shot blasting, chipping, riveting, drilling, camera flashing, power tools, IC engines, mixer machines, use of certain non-explosion proof equipment, vehicle entry or any other activities which may generate heat or spark.
items.value: B-6.14 Hot work shall include welding, grinding, gas cutting, burning, open flame, soldering, shot blasting, chipping, riveting, drilling, camera flashing, power tools, IC engines, mixer machines, use of certain non-explosion proof equipment, vehicle entry or any other activities which may generate heat or spark.

items.md: **B-6.15** Permit issuing authority, or any authorized officer can check the permit/stop the job and even cancel the permit on safety norms violation any time.
items.value: B-6.15 Permit issuing authority, or any authorized officer can check the permit/stop the job and even cancel the permit on safety norms violation any time.

items.md: **B-6.16** Enclose important Do's and Don'ts duly signed with each permit.
items.value: B-6.16 Enclose important Do's and Don'ts duly signed with each permit.

items.md: **B-6.17** All precautions given in permit must be clearly adhered by the permittee.
items.value: B-6.17 All precautions given in permit must be clearly adhered by the permittee.

items.md: **B-6.18** Daily toolbox talk/safety briefing to be ensured by the permittee before start of job. A register on toolbox talk shall be kept at site as record and signed by personnel associated with the work.
items.value: B-6.18 Daily toolbox talk/safety briefing to be ensured by the permittee before start of job. A register on toolbox talk shall be kept at site as record and signed by personnel associated with the work.

items.md: **B-6.19** Pre-use check of portable gas testers must be done to ensure its operability by GSI before the gas test at work site.
items.value: B-6.19 Pre-use check of portable gas testers must be done to ensure its operability by GSI before the gas test at work site.

B-7.1 Do's

items.md: **B-7.1.1** Ensure the availability of valid work permit and clearance before start of work.
items.value: B-7.1.1 Ensure the availability of valid work permit and clearance before start of work.

items.md: **B-7.1.2** Ensure that work permit conditions are fully complied at site.
items.value: B-7.1.2 Ensure that work permit conditions are fully complied at site.

items.md: **B-7.1.3** Area shall be free from all flammables and combustibles and the, drains etc, where there is likelihood of presence of hydrocarbon during the course of work shall be covered and sealed.
items.value: B-7.1.3 Area shall be free from all flammables and combustibles and the, drains etc, where there is likelihood of presence of hydrocarbon during the course of work shall be covered and sealed.

items.md: **B-7.1.4** In case of gas welding/cutting, cylinders should be kept in upright position and these should be easily identifiable by its colour.
items.value: B-7.1.4 In case of gas welding/cutting, cylinders should be kept in upright position and these should be easily identifiable by its colour.

items.md: **B-7.1.5** Hose connections of gas cylinders should be checked before starting of hot job. The hoses must be free from all cuts and kinks and distinct in color (maroon for acetylene and black for oxygen).
items.value: B-7.1.5 Hose connections of gas cylinders should be checked before starting of hot job. The hoses must be free from all cuts and kinks and distinct in color (maroon for acetylene and black for oxygen).

items.value: B-7.1.6 Executor must ensure use of proper PPEs during hot work.

items.value: B-7.1.8 Executer shall ensure use of flash back arrestor in gas cylinders while using oxy-acetylene sets. Use welding lighters only to light the welding torch.

items.md: ### C-14 DO’S AND DON’TS FOR WORKING IN CONFINED SPACE (To be attached with all Confined Space entry permits)
items.value: C-14 DO’S AND DON’TS FOR WORKING IN CONFINED SPACE (To be attached with all Confined Space entry permits)
Permit No.- [___]
C-14.1 Do’s

items.md: **C-14.1.1** Check for valid work permit “Entry into Confined Space” before entry into confined space.
items.value: C-14.1.1 Check for valid work permit “Entry into Confined Space” before entry into confined space.

items.md: **C-14.1.2** Ensure that the confined space is positively isolated from your source of energy and has been made free from harmful gases.
items.value: C-14.1.2 Ensure that the confined space is positively isolated from your source of energy and has been made free from harmful gases.

items.md: **C-14.1.3** Proper ventilation and illumination of space must be ensured.
items.value: C-14.1.3 Proper ventilation and illumination of space must be ensured.

items.md: **C-14.1.4** Wherever forced ventilation is used. The gas test should be carried out after minimum 10 min of stopping the eductor/fan/blower.
items.value: C-14.1.4 Wherever forced ventilation is used. The gas test should be carried out after minimum 10 min of stopping the eductor/fan/blower.

items.md: **C-14.1.5** Thorough gas test must be carried out before issuing permit to ensure that there is no pocket of gas available inside and the place is safe for performing the particular task inside confined space. For entry of person the concentration of toxic gases should not exceed Threshold Limit Value (TLV) and oxygen must be minimum 19.5 percent by volume.
items.value: C-14.1.5 Thorough gas test must be carried out before issuing permit to ensure that there is no pocket of gas available inside and the place is safe for performing the particular task inside confined space. For entry of person the concentration of toxic gases should not exceed Threshold Limit Value (TLV) and oxygen must be minimum 19.5 percent by volume.

items.md: **C-14.1.6** Use suitable respiratory protection for entry in the confined space, having oxygen deficiency or having toxic gases more than TLV.
items.value: C-14.1.6 Use suitable respiratory protection for entry in the confined space, having oxygen deficiency or having toxic gases more than TLV.

items.md: **C-14.1.7** Attendant should be available all the times at man way entry of confined space, till the work is in progress inside. The permit should be available with him and he shall maintain attendance in prescribed form.
items.value: C-14.1.7 Attendant should be available all the times at man way entry of confined space, till the work is in progress inside. The permit should be available with him and he shall maintain attendance in prescribed form.

items.md: **C-14.1.8** Attendant must be in constant touch with persons working inside and know the actions to be taken for rescue/emergency handling, if so required.
items.value: C-14.1.8 Attendant must be in constant touch with persons working inside and know the actions to be taken for rescue/emergency handling, if so required.

items.md: **C-14.1.9** The low voltage light limited to 24 V should be allowed inside confined space. All electric connection shall be provided with ELCB.
items.value: C-14.1.9 The low voltage light limited to 24 V should be allowed inside confined space. All electric connection shall be provided with ELCB.

items.md: **C-14.1.10** Wherever possible, lifelines with safety harness should be used for entry into confined space.
items.value: C-14.1.10 Wherever possible, lifelines with safety harness should be used for entry into confined space.

items.md: **C-14.1.11** Special precautions are required for entry into inert gas atmosphere. It requires the controlled condition inside confined space as well as mandatory use of compressed breathing air supply to person going inside with alternate air supply arrangement.
items.value: C-14.1.11 Special precautions are required for entry into inert gas atmosphere. It requires the controlled condition inside confined space as well as mandatory use of compressed breathing air supply to person going inside with alternate air supply arrangement.

items.md: **C-14.1.12** Keep at least 2 access/exit for each confined space, wherever possible. The access should be unobstructed and approachable.
items.value: C-14.1.12 Keep at least 2 access/exit for each confined space, wherever possible. The access should be unobstructed and approachable.

items.md: **C-14.1.13** Use required PPE and personal gas monitors while entering inside. Use of Safety helmet and safety shoe is mandatory for all work areas. Use air mask for areas, where there is likelihood of accumulation of gas during the work such as in sewers.
items.value: C-14.1.13 Use required PPE and personal gas monitors while entering inside. Use of Safety helmet and safety shoe is mandatory for all work areas. Use air mask for areas, where there is likelihood of accumulation of gas during the work such as in sewers.

items.md: **C-14.1.14** The person in confined space should pass the warning signal to stand by person in case of any suffocation/difficulty for help.
items.value: C-14.1.14 The person in confined space should pass the warning signal to stand by person in case of any suffocation/difficulty for help.

items.md: **C-14.1.15** Carry out regular gas test of confined spaces, where there is possibility of gas generation due to nature of work, being performed inside such as removal of sludge, hot works, etc.
items.value: C-14.1.15 Carry out regular gas test of confined spaces, where there is possibility of gas generation due to nature of work, being performed inside such as removal of sludge, hot works, etc.
C-14.2 Don’ts

items.md: **C-14.2.1** Do not use light of more than 24 V inside confined space.
items.value: C-14.2.1 Do not use light of more than 24 V inside confined space.

items.md: **C-14.2.2** Do not give any other responsibility to stand by person, which may cause his absence at man way.
items.value: C-14.2.2 Do not give any other responsibility to stand by person, which may cause his absence at man way.

items.md: **C-14.2.3** Do not obstruct the man ways from any side of confined space.
items.value: C-14.2.3 Do not obstruct the man ways from any side of confined space.

items.md: **D-25 INSTRUCTION**
items.value: D-25 INSTRUCTION

items.md: **D-25.1** The excavation clearance is required for all excavations including road and dyke cutting. Obtaining this clearance is prerequisite for EXCAVATION PERMIT.
items.value: D-25.1 The excavation clearance is required for all excavations including road and dyke cutting. Obtaining this clearance is prerequisite for EXCAVATION PERMIT.

items.md: **D-25.2** This clearance shall be obtained for a maximum period of 15 days, except at construction sites, non-operational areas, where it shall be for a maximum period of 1 month.
items.value: D-25.2 This clearance shall be obtained for a maximum period of 15 days, except at construction sites, non-operational areas, where it shall be for a maximum period of 1 month.

items.md: **D-25.3** This clearance shall be kept attached with excavation permit at site.
items.value: D-25.3 This clearance shall be kept attached with excavation permit at site.

items.md: **D-25.4** The clearance shall be issued in triplicate, original for permittee, first copy for issuer and second copy for fire station. The clearance shall be initiated by executer.
items.value: D-25.4 The clearance shall be issued in triplicate, original for permittee, first copy for issuer and second copy for fire station. The clearance shall be initiated by executer.

items.md: **D-25.5** On issue, original copy will be with executor, duplicate copy with fire station and triplicate copy with area in-charge/GSI (Gas Safety Inspector).
items.value: D-25.5 On issue, original copy will be with executor, duplicate copy with fire station and triplicate copy with area in-charge/GSI (Gas Safety Inspector).

items.md: **D-25.6** The clearance by area in-charge/GSI shall be given just before clearance by shift fire safety Officer (FSO) and registration at fire station. At last shift FSO will also check that clearances from all other dept. have been received.
items.value: D-25.6 The clearance by area in-charge/GSI shall be given just before clearance by shift fire safety Officer (FSO) and registration at fire station. At last shift FSO will also check that clearances from all other dept. have been received.

items.md: **D-25.7** This clearance shall be considered issued after registration at fire station.
items.value: D-25.7 This clearance shall be considered issued after registration at fire station.

items.md: **D-25.8** When job is completed, the receiver shall collect all the copies, and enclose them with closed copy of excavation permit before handing over to permit issuer. Permit issuer in turn after closing the permit will return the same to fire station.
items.value: D-25.8 When job is completed, the receiver shall collect all the copies, and enclose them with closed copy of excavation permit before handing over to permit issuer. Permit issuer in turn after closing the permit will return the same to fire station.

items.md: **D-25.9** For cutting of road in addition to this clearance, format for road closure and excavation permit shall be required. The road closure format shall be filled and submitted to fire station by the executor at least one day before starting the work.
items.value: D-25.9 For cutting of road in addition to this clearance, format for road closure and excavation permit shall be required. The road closure format shall be filled and submitted to fire station by the executor at least one day before starting the work.

items.md: **D-25.10** All road cuttings shall be properly barricaded with precautionary signs and alternate routes shall be displayed at the site by the executor.
items.value: D-25.10 All road cuttings shall be properly barricaded with precautionary signs and alternate routes shall be displayed at the site by the executor.

items.md: **D-25.11** Duly signed "Do's and Don'ts during excavation work" shall be integral part of excavation permit. All points mentioned in "Do's and Don'ts" must be complied at site.
items.value: D-25.11 Duly signed "Do's and Don'ts during excavation work" shall be integral part of excavation permit. All points mentioned in "Do's and Don'ts" must be complied at site.

items.value: Permit No.
D-26.1 Do's

items.md: **D-26.1.1** Ensure that clearance has been obtained from various departments in prescribed form for all excavation works.
items.value: D-26.1.1 Ensure that clearance has been obtained from various departments in prescribed form for all excavation works.

items.md: **D-26.1.2** Ensure that excavation permit and clearance has been obtained for excavation work and the same is valid.
items.value: D-26.1.2 Ensure that excavation permit and clearance has been obtained for excavation work and the same is valid.

items.md: **D-26.1.3** All excavations and open trenches must be barricaded around the opening. Warning lamp can be used for operating area and roads during darkness (use FLP type fixture in hazardous area).
items.value: D-26.1.3 All excavations and open trenches must be barricaded around the opening. Warning lamp can be used for operating area and roads during darkness (use FLP type fixture in hazardous area).

items.md: **D-26.1.4** There must be sufficient and safe means of access and escape for excavated sites.
items.value: D-26.1.4 There must be sufficient and safe means of access and escape for excavated sites.

items.md: **D-26.1.5** Excavation area should be free from fumes/gases heavier than air because it may cause asphyxiation or poisoning. All excavation of depth more than 1.2 m or more should be treated as confined space and permit for entry into confined space should be obtained.
items.value: D-26.1.5 Excavation area should be free from fumes/gases heavier than air because it may cause asphyxiation or poisoning. All excavation of depth more than 1.2 m or more should be treated as confined space and permit for entry into confined space should be obtained.

items.md: **D-26.1.6** Excavation to a depth of 1 m or more shall be cut stepped back to give a suitable slope or securely held by bracing so as to avoid the danger of side to collapse.
items.value: D-26.1.6 Excavation to a depth of 1 m or more shall be cut stepped back to give a suitable slope or securely held by bracing so as to avoid the danger of side to collapse.

items.md: **D-26.1.7** All trenches 1.2 m or more in depth shall at all times be equipped with at least one ladder for each 30.48 m length of excavation or fraction thereof. All such ladders shall be extended from bottom of the trench to at least 1 m above the surface of the ground.

D-26.2 Don’ts

items.md: **D-26.2.1** Under no circumstances under cutting to be carried out.
items.value: D-26.2.1 Under no circumstances under cutting to be carried out.

items.md: **D-26.2.2** Excavated earth shall not be dumped within 1 m of the edge of the excavation or depth the trench whichever is more.
items.value: D-26.2.2 Excavated earth shall not be dumped within 1 m of the edge of the excavation or depth the trench whichever is more.

items.md: **D-26.2.3** Excavated earth should not be dumped on nearby pathway/approaches, on product/steam line, in front of emergency facilities such as fire hydrants/monitor, fire alarm points, inlet of foam pourers etc and on OWS man hole covers.
items.value: D-26.2.3 Excavated earth should not be dumped on nearby pathway/approaches, on product/steam line, in front of emergency facilities such as fire hydrants/monitor, fire alarm points, inlet of foam pourers etc and on OWS man hole covers.

items.md: **D-26.2.4** No vehicle should be allowed to go too close to the edge of the excavation because it may cause side collapse. Maintain at least 2 m distances from the edge of the excavation. Adequate and well-anchored stop blocks should be provided on the surface to vehicle being driven reversed into the excavation.

items.md: **F-12.1** This permit will be issued in Triplicate. GREEN COPY (ORIGINAL) and WHITE COPY (TRIPLICATE) will be for the permittee. ISSUING AUTHORITY will retain WHITE COPY (DUPLICATE). This permit shall be valid for a period of 8 h only, unless it is renewed/extended. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of 7 calendar days.

items.md: **G-9 SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS**
items.value: G-9 SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS

items.md: **G-9.1** This permit shall be valid for a period of 8 h only, unless it is renewed/extended. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days.
items.value: G-9.1 This permit shall be valid for a period of 8 h only, unless it is renewed/extended. This clearance on the same permit can be renewed/extended up to a maximum of seven calendar days.

items.md: **G-9.2** In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency Sirens, all work must be stopped and all nonessential personnel must leave work site and processed to designated areas.
items.value: G-9.2 In case of fire alarm all work must immediately be stopped. In case of emergency Sirens, all work must be stopped and all nonessential personnel must leave work site and processed to designated areas.

items.md: **G-9.3** This permit must be available at work site at all times.
items.value: G-9.3 This permit must be available at work site at all times.

items.md: **G-9.4** This permit is to be made in duplicate : Blue and white colour. Blue copy is issued to permittee/ executor. The white copy is to be retained by issuing authority.
items.value: G-9.4 This permit is to be made in duplicate : Blue and white colour. Blue copy is issued to permittee/ executor. The white copy is to be retained by issuing authority.

items.md: **G-9.5** On completion of job, area to be cleared and copies of permit to be returned to the issuer after closing of permit. The same shall be closed by issuer and kept as record.
items.value: G-9.5 On completion of job, area to be cleared and copies of permit to be returned to the issuer after closing of permit. The same shall be closed by issuer and kept as record.

items.md: **G-9.6** All the columns in the form should be filled properly. No column should be left blank.
items.value: G-9.6 All the columns in the form should be filled properly. No column should be left blank.

items.md: **G-9.7** Announcement to be made and removal of nonessential persons are to be ensured from condoned off area before commencement of job. The barricading is to be done with warning tape with display of radiation hazard symbol at site by the site radiographer after carrying out the radiation survey.
items.value: G-9.7 Announcement to be made and removal of nonessential persons are to be ensured from condoned off area before commencement of job. The barricading is to be done with warning tape with display of radiation hazard symbol at site by the site radiographer after carrying out the radiation survey.

items.md: **G-9.8** The radiography should preferably be carried out at a time, when occupancy is least.
items.value: G-9.8 The radiography should preferably be carried out at a time, when occupancy is least.

items.md: **G-9.9** Radiation source movement within the site is to be maintained as per RSO guidelines.
items.value: G-9.9 Radiation source movement within the site is to be maintained as per RSO guidelines.

items.md: **G-9.10** The supervisor must be available at site during the radiography work.
items.value: G-9.10 The supervisor must be available at site during the radiography work.

items.md: **G-9.11** “Level-2 holder” means “radiological safety officer” designated by the employer and who in the opinion of the competent authority is qualified to discharge the functions under Rule 22 of *Atomic Energy (Radiation Protection) Rules, 2004*.
